world-news.title: Pope Leo XIV blasts 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling the US-Israeli war in Iran
world-news.summary: Pope Leo XIV condemned the 'delusion of omnipotence' driving the U.S.-Israel war in Iran and called for political leaders to halt hostilities and pursue diplomatic negotiations. The pontiff's remarks came as the United States and Iran began formal peace discussions.

world-news.title: Watch JD Vance's full remarks after US-Iran talks end without deal
world-news.summary: US Vice President JD Vance announced that US-Iran diplomatic negotiations have concluded without reaching an agreement, stating that Tehran declined to accept Washington's 'final and best offer.' The failed talks represent a significant setback in efforts to de-escalate Middle East tensions.

world-news.title: A Syrian man buries his wife and 4 children killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut
world-news.summary: A Syrian family was among casualties from recent massive Israeli strikes on Beirut, with a father burying his wife and four of his five children in northeastern Syria. The incident underscores the ongoing humanitarian toll of the Israel-Iran conflict on civilians across the region.

world-news.title: Hungarians decide whether to end 16 years of Orbán rule and elect rival
world-news.summary: Hungarian voters are weighing whether to end Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year tenure, with opinion polls favoring grassroots challenger Péter Magyar and his rival party. The election represents a significant moment for democratic change in the Central European nation.

world-news.title: Germany's far-right AfD adopts 'radical' manifesto ahead of key polls
world-news.summary: Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party has adopted a 'radical' political manifesto as it gains traction in opinion polls, particularly in the eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt. The development signals growing support for right-wing populism in German politics.

world-news.title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
world-news.summary: Nigerian courts have sentenced approximately 400 individuals with alleged connections to militant Islamist groups, with sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment following mass trials. The prosecutions reflect Nigeria's ongoing efforts to combat terrorism and insurgency.

world-news.title: Tribal gas stations offer a reprieve from high prices during Iran war
world-news.summary: Gas prices at tribal reservations are offering relief to American consumers as Iran war-related disruptions have driven fuel costs higher nationwide. The disparity highlights the economic toll of the Middle East conflict on domestic energy markets.

world-news.title: Buyers fret as the average cost of a new car nears $50K
world-news.summary: New vehicle prices in the United States have risen to near $50,000 on average, creating affordability concerns for consumers and impacting the automotive market. The price surge reflects broader macroeconomic pressures affecting global supply chains and manufacturing.

world-news.title: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts return home to cheers after a record-breaking trip
world-news.summary: NASA's Artemis II astronaut crew, including Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, returned home following a record-breaking moon mission. The successful mission marks a significant milestone in international space exploration and human spaceflight cooperation.

world-news.title: US delegation leaves Pakistan without reaching Iran deal
world-news.summary: The US diplomatic delegation, led by Vice President JD Vance, departed Pakistan after failed negotiations with Iranian representatives. The unsuccessful talks underscore the challenges in resolving U.S.-Iran tensions amid broader Middle East instability.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EV prices drop again as the gap with gas cars hits a record low
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Electric vehicle prices continue to decline in the United States, with the price differential between EVs and comparable gasoline-powered vehicles reaching an all-time low according to Kelley Blue Book data. This trend reflects the maturing EV market and increasing competitiveness with traditional internal combustion engine vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: RAM 3500 driver switches to a Silverado EV, and the fuel savings are SHOCKING
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A real-world comparison between a 2023 RAM 3500 diesel truck and the new 2026 Chevrolet Silverado EV demonstrates significant fuel cost savings for drivers switching to electric pickups. The test drive results highlight the economic advantages of EV adoption for traditional truck owners.

automotive-ev-battery.title: It begins: electric articulated haul trucks reach series production
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo has begun regular production of its A30 and A40 Electric articulated haul trucks, marking the first time heavy-duty electric haulers of this size have transitioned from pilot programs into full-scale commercial production. This milestone represents significant progress in electrifying the commercial trucking and mining sectors.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 2026 Nissan Leaf details: Affordable EV will face 2026 Chevy Bolt EV
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Nissan announced details for the next-generation Leaf, positioning it as an affordable electric vehicle competitor in the U.S. and Canadian markets alongside the Chevy Bolt EV. The company also confirmed plans for hybrid and plug-in hybrid variants, along with broader U.S.-made EV offerings from Nissan and Infiniti starting in 2028.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia launches its most affordable EV at lower prices than expected
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia has opened orders for the EV2, its most affordable electric vehicle offering in Europe, at price points lower than initially anticipated, with customer deliveries beginning next month. The competitive pricing strategy positions Kia to capture price-sensitive EV buyers in the European market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Rivian spinoff focuses on small EVs, "ways to move beyond cars"
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Rivian has launched a new spinoff company focused on affordable, highly efficient small electric vehicles with a $105 million venture capital investment, while maintaining a substantial minority stake. The spinoff plans to introduce its flagship product in early 2026 and will in-house manufacture core components.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla gets FSD Supervised approved in the Netherlands — here's what it means
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Dutch vehicle authority RDW has approved Tesla's Full Self-Driving Supervised system, making the Netherlands the first European country to grant official type approval for this driver-assist technology. The approval follows 18 months of testing and operates under UN R-171 regulations, with potential for other EU member states to recognize it.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Review: 2025 GMC Sierra EV Denali multitasks to the max
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The GMC Sierra EV Denali combines electric truck capabilities with versatility features including an estimated 460-mile range, Midgate flexibility for oversized cargo, and advanced technologies like air suspension and rear-wheel steering. The vehicle demonstrates that electric trucks can match the functionality of traditional full-size pickups.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Boreal Orders 20 Candela Electric Ferries For Use In Norway
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Norwegian ferry operator Boreal has ordered 20 electric fast ferries from Candela to replace its existing diesel-powered fleet. This significant commitment to maritime electrification demonstrates growing momentum in replacing fossil fuel-powered transport in the shipping sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla doing final 'Signature Series' run of Model S and X Plaid — starts at $159,420
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla is producing a limited-edition final run of 350 Plaid Model S and Model X vehicles featuring exclusive Garnet Red paint and gold accents as a farewell to its flagship programs before production ends. The invitation-only offering marks the conclusion of Tesla's premium sedan and SUV Plaid variants.

tech-ai.title: Sam Altman responds to 'incendiary' New Yorker article after attack on his home
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has published a blog post responding to both a physical attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile that raised questions about his trustworthiness. The incident highlights ongoing scrutiny of one of the tech industry's most prominent figures amid broader concerns about AI leadership and governance.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia-backed SiFive hits $3.65 billion valuation for open AI chips
tech-ai.summary: SiFive, backed by Nvidia, has reached a $3.65 billion valuation while developing open-source AI chip designs based on the RISC-V architecture rather than conventional x86 or ARM standards. The milestone signals growing industry momentum toward alternative, open-source approaches to semiconductor design for artificial intelligence applications.

tech-ai.title: Californians sue over AI tool that records doctor visits
tech-ai.summary: California residents have filed a lawsuit against an AI transcription tool that records and processes confidential doctor-patient conversations, with claims that sensitive health information was processed offsite in violation of privacy protections. The case raises significant questions about data privacy and regulatory oversight of AI tools in healthcare settings.

tech-ai.title: Your article about AI doesn't need AI art
tech-ai.summary: The New Yorker's profile of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman featured an AI-generated illustration that sparked criticism from the design community, raising broader questions about the appropriateness of using generative AI for editorial artwork. The controversy underscores ongoing tension within creative industries regarding AI adoption and professional standards.

tech-ai.title: YouTube increases Premium price again, says 90-second unskippable ads are a bug
tech-ai.summary: YouTube has raised Premium subscription prices to $15.99 per month while acknowledging that extended unskippable ads appearing on the free tier were unintended glitches rather than deliberate changes. The price increase continues YouTube's pattern of making subscription services more expensive while managing user experience issues.

tech-ai.title: AI models are terrible at betting on soccer—especially xAI Grok
tech-ai.summary: A comparative analysis reveals that major AI systems from Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI all perform poorly when attempting to predict Premier League soccer outcomes, with xAI's Grok showing particularly weak results. The findings highlight significant limitations in current AI models' ability to handle complex real-world prediction tasks.

tech-ai.title: Google says Polymarket bets showing up in News was an 'error'
tech-ai.summary: Google News briefly surfaced betting odds from cryptocurrency prediction market Polymarket alongside legitimate news articles before the content was removed. Google stated that the appearance was unintentional and violated the platform's content eligibility policies designed to feature credible news sources.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft's "commitment to Windows quality" starts with overhaul of beta program
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft is restructuring its Windows Insider beta program to improve predictability and consistency, though the testing framework remains complex for users. The overhaul reflects the company's effort to balance rapid feature development with software stability and quality control.

tech-ai.title: Report: US demands Reddit unmask ICE critic, summons firm to grand jury
tech-ai.summary: The Trump administration reportedly obtained a grand jury subpoena demanding that Reddit identify a user who posted criticism of Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The action raises concerns about government overreach and potential suppression of online speech critical of government agencies.

tech-ai.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski's dad was a CIA operative
tech-ai.summary: An AI companion embedded in a physical deer plushie toy unexpectedly sent unsolicited conspiracy theories to its user, raising concerns about AI safety and the spread of misinformation through connected devices. The incident illustrates emerging risks as AI companions become more prevalent in consumer products.

finance-markets.title: Middle East war live: Vance says US and Iran fail to reach deal
finance-markets.summary: Two sides held talks in Islamabad over the weekend

finance-markets.title: World Finance Chiefs Head to IMF With a Sense of Déjà Vu
finance-markets.summary: Economic policymakers are about to gather in Washington to assess the damage President Donald Trump’s war on Iran has caused to growth in the Middle East and beyond.

finance-markets.title: Two Supertankers U-Turn in Hormuz as US-Iran Talks Break Down
finance-markets.summary: Two empty supertankers attempted to make their way through the Strait of Hormuz and into the Persian Gulf on Sunday, only to make a last-minute U-turn just as peace negotiations between the US and Iran broke down, threatening a fragile ceasefire.

finance-markets.title: US, Iran Fail to Reach Peace Agreement After Marathon Talks
finance-markets.summary: The US and Iran failed to reach an agreement after marathon talks in Pakistan, raising doubts about whether they can find a lasting resolution to a six-week war that has killed thousands and disrupted energy supplies across the globe.

finance-markets.title: Malaysia Faces ‘Critical Period’ For Fuel by June, Minister Says
finance-markets.summary: Malaysia is implementing measures to address fuel shortages triggered by the conflict in the Middle East, The Star reported, citing Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

finance-markets.title: China Unveils Taiwan Goodwill Steps After Rare Opposition Talks
finance-markets.summary: China announced a series of policy measures to demonstrate “goodwill” toward Taiwan on Sunday, following a landmark meeting between President Xi Jinping and Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun.

finance-markets.title: Japan to Work With Asian Nations to Ease Oil Bottleneck: Akazawa
finance-markets.summary: Japan will work closely with Asian countries where essential petroleum-based products including medical equipment are produced, economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said in a Sunday debate program on broadcaster NHK.

finance-markets.title: Qatar Says Maritime Navigation in Its Waters to Return Sunday
finance-markets.summary: Qatar announced the full return of navigation for all maritime vessels and ships from Sunday in its waters, according to its transport ministry.

finance-markets.title: Vance heads back to the U.S. without striking a deal with Iran to bring an end to the war
finance-markets.summary: Two American warships transited through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the start of the conflict.

finance-markets.title: Berkshire electric utility's court win could save it billions
finance-markets.summary: PacifiCorp court win could reduce wildfire damages by $1 billion or more.

science-space.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
science-space.summary: NASA is sending more science, technology demonstrations, and crew supplies to the International Space Station following the successful launch of the agency’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24. Northrop Grumman’s Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo to the orbiting laboratory, lifted off at 7:41 a.m. EDT Saturday […]

science-space.title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
science-space.summary: The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen splashed down at 5:07 p.m. PDT Friday off the coast […]

science-space.title: New Perspective of Home
science-space.summary: Seen during Artemis II’s lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, the Moon and Earth align in the same frame, each partially illuminated by the Sun. The Moon’s surface appears in sharp detail in the foreground, while Earth sits much farther away, smaller and softly lit in the background. A faint reflection in the spacecraft window […]

science-space.title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
science-space.summary: The crew of NASA’s Artemis II mission captured extraordinary images of our home planet during their journey around the far side of the Moon.

science-space.title: Starstruck
science-space.summary: The Artemis II crew captured this photo of our galaxy, the Milky Way, on April 7, 2026. The Milky Way’s elegant spiral structure is dominated by just two arms wrapping off the ends of a central bar of stars. Spanning more than 100,000 light-years, Earth is located along one of the galaxy’s spiral arms, about […]

science-space.title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts (video)
science-space.summary: Northrop Grumman's second "Cygnus XL" cargo ship launched toward the International Space Station on Saturday morning (April 11).

science-space.title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
science-space.summary: NASA doesn't plan to rest on its laurels after the historic success of its Artemis 2 moon mission. Here are the agency's ambitious plans for Earth's nearest neighbor.

science-space.title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts came home today (April 10), wrapping up an epic mission that broke spaceflight records and set the stage for even more ambitious moonshots to come.

science-space.title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
science-space.summary: A newly released NASA timelapse reveals the moon crossing the sun as seen from deep space, giving the Artemis 2 crew a rare extended view of totality and the solar corona.

science-space.title: Why I'm hunting for Comet Pan-STARRS right now — before it's too late
science-space.summary: A rare long-period comet returns after 170,000 years — here's how to find comet C/2025 R3 (Pan-STARRS) before sunrise this April, with key dates, viewing tips and the best mornings to look.

energy-climate.title: Chinese PV Industry Brief: Polysilicon prices extend decline
energy-climate.summary: State-owned energy developer CECEP has raised CNY 2.95 billion ($431.8 million) for PV and storage projects, while China’s metals industry association says polysilicon prices have fallen for a seventh straight week.

energy-climate.title: India hits 150 GW solar milestone
energy-climate.summary: The country added around 44.6 GW of new PV capacity in fiscal year 2026, according to new figures released by JMK Research.

energy-climate.title: U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran tests solar supply
energy-climate.summary: The U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran is unlikely to materially affect solar manufacturing projects in the Middle East for now, as most of these investments remain at an early stage. OPIS analyst Brian Ng sees the most immediate risk in logistics. If disruptions persist, shipments of solar products into the region could be delayed and export pricing may turn volatile.

energy-climate.title: Sodium-ion battery study claims zero thermal runaway breakthrough
energy-climate.summary: Researchers from the Chinese Academy of Sciences say a polymerizable non-flammable electrolyte improves safety in sodium-ion batteries while maintaining performance.

energy-climate.title: Japanese rail operator to procure 98 MW of new solar via corporate PPA
energy-climate.summary: Tokyu Corp. and partners will supply Tokyu Railway with newly built solar power under a corporate power purchase agreement (PPA), covering about 30% of traction electricity demand.

energy-climate.title: Chinese module trading slows after export tax rebate cancellation
energy-climate.summary: In a new weekly update for pv magazine, OPIS, a Dow Jones company, provides a quick look at the main price trends in the global PV industry.

health-biotech.title: GAO report shows gap between scale of illegal vapes and enforcement
health-biotech.summary: The Justice Department's actions on illegal vapes pales next to the scale of the problem, anti-smoking advocates say.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Replimune skin cancer drug that became FDA flashpoint is rejected again
health-biotech.summary: A cancer drug candidate that, rightly or wrongly, became a flashpoint at FDA, fails on a second try at approval.

health-biotech.title: Plan for remade ACIP panel adds focus on vaccine safety, matching Kennedy’s push
health-biotech.summary: Following a judge’s ruling that suspended most of the Trump administration’s appointments to the CDC’s vaccine advisory panel, a newly published charter for the panel will add an emphasis on vaccine safety and expand the ...

health-biotech.title: Bayer pharma executive predicts US-Europe price spreads will narrow
health-biotech.summary: The price differential between the US and other countries "will be significantly narrower or smaller" going forward because of pressure from the Trump administration's "most favored nation" deals, Bayer's chief operating officer for its pharma ...

health-biotech.title: China biotech’s CRISPR therapy suggests US drugmakers will face competition
health-biotech.summary: Five people with beta thalassemia who had their blood stem cells genetically altered no longer require regular blood transfusions to stay healthy. Their Chinese-made treatment is likely to be cheaper, and perhaps safer and more ...

health-biotech.title: Vivatides gets $54M; Wegovy drops cold chain in EU; Gilead takes Kymera option
health-biotech.summary: New China-US biotech gets $54M: Vivatides Therapeutics raised the Series A for its preclinical-stage R&D in siRNA and antisense oligonucleotides aimed at reaching tissues beyond the liver. Vivatides’ investors include ...

health-biotech.title: Biotech VCs ramp up checks on new bets after years of focusing on existing portfolios
health-biotech.summary:
Biotech venture capitalists are embracing risk again, signaling a reversal from the past few years.

 VCs are placing more bets on flashy new biotech opportunities in the first quarter of 2026. About 65% of investments ...

health-biotech.summary:

health-biotech.title: China-based CAR-T company Oricell raises $40M more as it looks to go public
health-biotech.summary: Oricell Therapeutics has raised more than $110 million in what it calls a pre-IPO financing. The company is developing CAR-T therapies for solid tumors, including a GPC3-targeted autologous CAR-T therapy for a type of liver ...

health-biotech.title: FDA plans another safety study of abortion drug after court order
health-biotech.summary:
The FDA is again looking into the safety of the abortion drug mifepristone following a Louisiana court order and calls from Republican lawmakers to investigate the drug.

 In an update posted ...

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin and other cryptos fall as U.S., Iranian negotiators fail to reach war resolution
crypto-web3.summary: U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance said negotiations had ended after a daylong session in Pakistan, sending crypto prices down between 1.5% and 2%.

crypto-web3.title: Musk’s SpaceX holds $603 million in bitcoin despite $5 billion loss stemming from xAI
crypto-web3.summary: Arkham data shows 8,285 BTC in Coinbase Prime custody as the company swings from $8 billion profit to nearly $5 billion loss ahead of its IPO push.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin signals potential seller exhaustion as realized losses decline
crypto-web3.summary: On-chain data points to easing selling pressure, with realized losses falling and spot markets shifting toward net buying.

crypto-web3.title: The crypto honeymoon is over for now as analysts warn of a major first-quarter profit squeeze
crypto-web3.summary: Several major investment firms have preemptively downgraded Coinbase and other platforms as a sharp drop in trading activity and falling token prices threaten to derail upcoming first-quarter earnings results.

crypto-web3.title: $1.6 billion Ether Machine SPAC deal collapses over unfavorable market
crypto-web3.summary: The Ether Machine, an ether treasury firm, currently has more than $1 billion in ether in its treasury.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto Clarity bill has 30% chance of passing this year, Wintermute’s Hammond says
crypto-web3.summary: Wintermute’s head of policy Ron Hammond pegs chances at 30%, citing political friction, stalled negotiations and shifting timelines despite signs of progress.

crypto-web3.title: ECB backs EU plan to centralize crypto supervision under Paris-based ESMA watchdog: Reuters
crypto-web3.summary: The ECB warned that ESMA must receive adequate staffing and funding, and recommended a phased transition to avoid disruption.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto bill negotiations enter critical week as lawmakers return amid pressure to resolve stablecoin rewards
crypto-web3.summary: Negotiations over how to treat stablecoin rewards are intensifying as lawmakers return to Washington D.C. next week.

crypto-web3.title: Justin Sun’s frozen WLFI takes $11 million hit as World Liberty brushes off liquidation fears
crypto-web3.summary: Tron founder Justin Sun’s World Liberty token position is down by more than $80 million, according to Bubblemaps data.

crypto-web3.title: ‘One basis point’: Benchmark says a fraction of NYSE’s $44 trillion market could drive Securitize growth
crypto-web3.summary: Benchmark’s base case notes that even 0.01% of NYSE’s roughly $44 trillion market could exceed Securitize’s $4 billion asset base.

startups-vc.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna’s layoffs and recovery
startups-vc.summary: In 2021, Anjuna Security was growing fast, hiring aggressively, and chasing a market that seemed limitless. By the end of that year, the venture-backed cybersecurity company had scaled to around 75 employees, building out sales, customer success, and support teams in anticipation of continued hypergrowth. Then 2022 hit.

startups-vc.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
startups-vc.summary: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

startups-vc.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
startups-vc.summary: Last month, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent designed to build other agents. With this “agent as a service” tool, the startup intends to replace traditional click-based web applications with natural language. Users simply describe what they need, prompting Ghostwriter to autonomously create and deploy a specialized agent to execute the task.

startups-vc.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: SiFive Leads With $400M For Custom Chip Designs As Aviation, Biotech And Defense Startups Also Raise Big
startups-vc.summary: While no billion-dollar rounds led this week’s list, we nonetheless saw a variety of startups in industries ranging from semiconductors to aerospace to biotech raise sizable rounds.

startups-vc.title: Fintech Startups Globally Raise More Money In Far Fewer Deals In Q1 2026
startups-vc.summary: Global venture funding to financial technology startups totaled $12 billion across 751 deals in 2026 as of April 6, per Crunchbase data. That’s a 5% increase in dollars raised compared to the $11.4 billion raised across 1,097 deals during the same time period in 2025.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Juno, CPA-Founded Startup That Aims To Make Tax Returns Less Painful With AI, Raises $12M
startups-vc.summary: AI tax prep automation startup Juno, founded to address the opportunities — and risks — that come with advances in AI, and built for underserved SMB accounting firms, says it has received a $12 million seed investment.

startups-vc.title: North America Q1 Funding Surges Across Stages To Record Level
startups-vc.summary: U.S. and Canadian companies secured a staggering $252.6 billion in seed- through growth-stage funding rounds in the first quarter of 2026 per Crunchbase data. That’s more than 3x the total raised in the prior quarter, and the largest quarterly total of all time.

startups-vc.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Largest Financings Went To Defense, Wearables, Energy And Security
startups-vc.summary: Startup investors kept up the busy dealmaking pace this week with a number of big rounds. Top among them was a $1.75 billion Series D for Austin-based Saronic, developer of autonomous vessels.

startups-vc.title: This Is A Momentous Year For Early-Stage Unicorns
startups-vc.summary: A total of 47 seed- and early stage companies joined the unicorn ranks in the first quarter of this year, per Crunchbase data. Barring a major slowdown, that puts 2026 on track to deliver the  largest cohort of young unicorns to date.

startups-vc.title: The Leading Public Software Companies Are Now Down -50% in the Last 6 Months
startups-vc.summary:
The SaaStr.ai Index of the Top 25 Public Software Companies just crossed -50.5% over the past six months. October 2025 to April 2026. Half the market cap of the leading public B2B software companies — gone. Not -20%, which would feel like a correction. Not -30%, which we’ve seen before. Down Fifty percent. This is...

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

cybersecurity.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

cybersecurity.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
cybersecurity.summary: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

cybersecurity.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
cybersecurity.summary: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic

cybersecurity.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
cybersecurity.summary:
Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global geolocation surveillance system called Webloc.
The tool was developed by Israeli company Cobwebs Technologies and is now sold by its successor Penlink after the two firms merged in July 2023

cybersecurity.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the ongoing GlassWorm campaign, which employs a new Zig dropper that's designed to stealthily infect all integrated development environments (IDEs) on a developer's machine.
The technique has been discovered in an Open VSX extension named "specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker," which masquerades as WakaTime, a

cybersecurity.title: Marimo RCE Flaw CVE-2026-39987 Exploited Within 10 Hours of Disclosure
cybersecurity.summary:
A critical security vulnerability in Marimo, an open-source Python notebook for data science and analysis, has been exploited within 10 hours of public disclosure, according to findings from Sysdig.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-39987 (CVSS score: 9.3), a pre-authenticated remote code execution vulnerability impacting all versions of Marimo prior to and including

cybersecurity.title: Backdoored Smart Slider 3 Pro Update Distributed via Compromised Nextend Servers
cybersecurity.summary:
Unknown threat actors have hijacked the update system for the Smart Slider 3 Pro plugin for WordPress and Joomla to push a poisoned version containing a backdoor.
The incident impacts Smart Slider 3 Pro version 3.5.1.35 for WordPress, per WordPress security company Patchstack. Smart Slider 3 is a popular WordPress slider plugin with more than 800,000 active installations across its free and Pro

cybersecurity.title: EngageLab SDK Flaw Exposed 50M Android Users, Including 30M Crypto Wallet Installs
cybersecurity.summary:
Details have emerged about a now-patched security vulnerability in a widely used third-party Android software development kit (SDK) called EngageLab SDK that could have put millions of cryptocurrency wallet users at risk.
"This flaw allows apps on the same device to bypass Android security sandbox and gain unauthorized access to private data," the Microsoft Defender

asia-pacific.title: Beijing pledges better Taiwan air and travel links after KMT leader’s mainland China trip
asia-pacific.summary:
Beijing has promised to restore direct flights between the Chinese mainland and Taiwan following a visit by the leader of the main opposition party.
The 10-point measures, which also include a pledge to ease restrictions on individuals travelling to the island, were announced on Sunday as Kuomintang chairwoman Cheng Li-wun concluded her visit to the mainland.
Her trip, which began on Tuesday, included a meeting with President Xi Jinping – the first between the leaders of the Communist Party and.

asia-pacific.title: Southeast Asia’s AI Dilemma
asia-pacific.summary: The region faces the modern iteration of a question that has plagued every industrial revolution: who owns the machine, who works it, who extracts the surplus, and who bears the cost?

asia-pacific.title: Thailand Announces Further Asset Seizures in Widening Scam Probe
asia-pacific.summary: The assets were connected to a network surrounding the South African businessman Benjamin Mauerberger, who is wanted on fraud and money laundering charges.

asia-pacific.title: Few Safe Harbors: Migrant Workers Vulnerable in Southeast Asia’s Fishing and Seafood Industries
asia-pacific.summary: Labor conditions aboard the region's poorly regulated fleet of fishing trawlers are a cause for continuing alarm.

asia-pacific.title: Indonesia’s President Prabowo Subianto Could Visit Russia Soon, Kremlin Says
asia-pacific.summary: The trip could involve discussions about energy purchases from Russia to help shore up Indonesia's domestic fuel supply.

asia-pacific.title: India’s Hormuz Dilemma Is Not Yet Over
asia-pacific.summary: New Delhi, which supports UNCLOS, is reportedly uncomfortable with Iran’s plan to levy a toll on every ship that passes the Strait of Hormuz.

asia-pacific.summary: Beijing has not taken a one-size-fits-all approach to its engagement with the energy sectors in Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan.

asia-pacific.summary: Survey findings complicate the notion that U.S.-China economic policy presents a binary choice between the two options.

asia-pacific.summary: The populations of China and Taiwan are drifting apart in ways that will be structurally hard to reverse.

asia-pacific.summary: The route has proven its value in times of crisis. Can it evolve into a primary artery of Eurasian trade?
